project.objective: When and where was Robert Raymond McKee born?

questions.question: When and where was Robert Raymond McKee (son of Robert McKee and Margaret Pollock, resident Newry, County Down, Ireland in 1911, died 1958 Chicago) born?
questions.rationale: This is the direct GPS question matching the project objective. L8K8-W12 has no birth fact in the tree. His parents married June 1890 in Newry, County Down; he resided there in 1911 and immigrated to North America in 1923. Irish civil registration (births registered from 1864) and Ireland census records (1901, 1911) are the primary repositories; church registers for the Newry area are also available on FamilySearch. A birth circa 1890–1910 in County Down is well within surviving record coverage.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: All plan items completed or skipped. Civil registration index (derivative, primary informant) establishes Q1 1904, Banbridge, County Down — corroborated by 1911 census (original). Absence from 1901 census provides independent negative evidence consistent with post-April 1901 birth. Immigration self-reports (1923) form a single evidence unit (same informant); birth-year conflict c_001 resolved in favor of the civil registration index. Catholic parish registers skipped (Unitarian household confirmed 1911). IrishGenealogy.ie fallback skipped (pli_002 succeeded; original adds precision only; overturn risk negligible with two independent sources already in agreement).

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: Yes — two independent sources establish Q1 1904, Banbridge, County Down, Ireland as the answer to 'when and where was Robert Raymond McKee born?' Conflict c_001 is resolved. The question is answered at a defensible tier.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: FamilySearch civil registration indexes (collection 1408347), Ireland Census 1901 (1626180), Ireland Census 1911 (2854327), three 1923 immigration/border-crossing records, and WWII draft registration all searched. Catholic parish registers skipped as inapplicable (Unitarian household). IrishGenealogy.ie external site skipped as the FamilySearch civil index fallback condition was not triggered. Name variants M'kee, McKee, Mc Kee covered by the FamilySearch search without birthPlace filter.
stop_criteria.original_substitution: The civil registration index (src_001) is a derivative; the original GRO birth register is accessible on IrishGenealogy.ie (pli_007) but was skipped because the derivative finding is independently corroborated by the original 1911 census (src_002). The 1911 census is an original source providing primary corroboration; the derivative index is not the sole evidence for the conclusion.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: Two independent evidence units: (1) Civil registration index (src_001) — parents as informants at registration, proximate to the event; (2) Ireland Census 1911 (src_002) — original census schedule, enumerator-collected, independent informant chain. A third unit (1901 census absence, src_003) provides negative corroboration. Immigration self-reports (src_004/005/006) are one unit (same informant — the subject) and support Banbridge as birthplace.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: src_001 is a derivative source with primary information (parent-informants at registration). src_002 is an original source with indeterminate information (pre-1940 census; informant unknown). At least one original source with corroborating information is present.
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: c_001 (birth year conflict: Q1 1904 vs. 1900/1903/Nov 1900 from immigration records) is resolved, preferred assertion a_002. No remaining unresolved conflicts.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: Low. The only unsearched accessible source is the original GRO birth register on IrishGenealogy.ie, which would most plausibly confirm Q1 1904, Banbridge — not introduce a different quarter or district. The two independent sources (civil index + 1911 census) are in agreement. WWII draft card image was mislinked in FamilySearch (different individual); indexed data for QGF4-CZQH cannot be verified from the available image and was not used as evidence.

items.rationale: Three immigration/border-crossing records already sourced in the tree (St. Albans Canadian Border Crossings 9YN8-JHB, Canada Immigration Records Q36D-6VF, US Border Crossings 9YNC-RDY) have not yet been read for birth-date and birthplace assertions. St. Albans manifests and Canadian Form 30A routinely recorded age, birth year, and country of origin; some list the exact birthplace. Reading these first extracts the most immediately available birth evidence without a new search.

Ireland, Civil Registration, 1845-1913 (collection 2659409) is the primary authoritative source for the subject's birth. Irish civil registration began in 1864; births were recorded by district registrar in the Newry Superintendent Registrar's District, County Down. A civil birth entry would give the exact date, place (townland), and father's name — direct evidence for both the date and place objective. Search for 'Robert McKee' born Newry district, County Down, circa 1890-1910.

Ireland Census 1901 (collection 1626180) is fully indexed and survived intact. Robert McKee (LT1G-QP2) and Margaret Pollock married 1890 in Newry; Robert Raymond McKee should appear as a child in their household circa age 0-11. The census return gives each member's age, relationship, county of birth, and religion — indirect but corroborating evidence for birth year and birthplace county.

Ireland Census 1911 (collection 2854327) confirms Robert Raymond McKee was in Newry in 1911 (tree fact). The original census schedule gives his exact stated age (and thus birth year), county of birth, and religion — important corroborating evidence. The 1911 census also uniquely records how many years each couple had been married and how many children born/living, helping profile the family.

items.jurisdiction: Stockton, San Joaquin, California, United States
items.rationale: Robert R. McKee registered for the WWII draft on 15 Feb 1942 in Stockton, CA (tree fact). Series III draft registration cards recorded the registrant's exact birth date and birthplace — often the most precise US-held record of a foreign-born person's birth. FamilySearch holds the WWII Draft Registration Cards collection. This corroborates or refines what the immigration records say.

Ireland, Catholic Parish Registers, 1740-1900 (collection 2820100) was planned on the assumption of Catholic background (from the Archdiocese of Chicago cemetery record). However, the Ireland Census 1911 (src_002) records the McKee household as 'Unitarian,' not Catholic. The cemetery affiliation reflects the burial institution's denomination, not the decedent's religion. Catholic parish baptism registers are inapplicable for a Unitarian family. No yield expected; skipping.

IrishGenealogy.ie was designated as fallback_for pli_002. pli_002 succeeded: civil registration index (ark:/61903/1:1:FB2F-WZ8) was found in FamilySearch collection 1408347, establishing Q1 1904, Banbridge, County Down as the registration quarter and district. The original GRO birth register image on IrishGenealogy.ie would add the exact birth date within Q1 1904 and the townland, but would not change the conclusion for the research question ('when and where born'). The finding is independently corroborated by the 1911 census (src_002, original source). Overturn risk from the original is negligible: the distinctive middle name 'Raymond,' Banbridge district, and Q1 1904 are already established by two independent sources. Skipping as the fallback condition (pli_002 failure) did not occur and the original adds precision only.

log.notes: US border crossing Nov 1923 from Montreal: Robert R McKee, birth Nov 1900, birth place Banbridge [County Down, Ireland]; race Irish; prior residence Sask [Saskatchewan]. Image at ark via sd_da1 not stored. Two of three records now agree on Banbridge as birthplace; birth year conflict: 1900 vs 1903.

One hit: Robert McKee born 7 Jul 1900, Belfast, Ireland. Father Robert McKee, mother Sarah McKee. Not our subject — mother should be Margaret Pollock, birthplace should be Banbridge or Newry area per immigration records. Match score 0.008 (subject unresolvable — no birth date in tree). Retrying with birthPlace=Banbridge and broader year range.

Zero results when filtering coll 2659409 to Banbridge area. Suspect place filter too restrictive or record indexed under different district. Parallel search of collection 1408347 (Civil Registration Indexes) found Robert Raymond M'kee born Jan-Mar 1904, Banbridge, County Down — 4-star tree match to L8K8-W12.

Key hit: ark:/61903/1:1:FB2F-WZ8 — Robert Raymond M'kee, BirthRegistration Q1 (Jan-Mar) 1904, Banbridge, County Down, Ireland. Already attached to subject L8K8-W12 (4-star tree match). This is a civil registration index entry (derivative); original GRO register entry needed for exact birth date. Record ranked 2nd by matcher (score 0.025) but is clearly the correct subject given distinctive middle name 'Raymond' and Banbridge birthplace consistent with 2 of 3 immigration records.

Found 'Robt McKee' (LT1G-QP2, 5-star match) as head of household in Newry, Banbridge, County Down, 1901 — this is the subject's father. Robert Raymond McKee himself does not appear in 1901 census because he was not yet born (civil registration index shows Q1 1904 birth). Absence from 1901 census is consistent with and supports a 1904 birth date.

Key hit: ark:/61903/1:1:QYXF-J52M — 'Robert Raymond McKee', Scholar, Unitarian, born 1904 County Down, Ireland, in household of Robert McKee, Newry, Banbridge, County Down, Ireland. 5-star tree match to L8K8-W12. Confirms 1904 birth year and County Down birthplace. Household head is the subject's father (LT1G-QP2). Census taken April 1911; subject was approximately 7 years old, consistent with Q1 1904 birth.

Top hit: ark:/61903/1:1:QGF4-CZQH — 'Robert Raymond McKee', MilitaryDraftRegistration 15 Feb 1942, Stockton CA. Birth date indexed as 29 February 1904 (1904 was a leap year; date is valid). Birth place indexed as 'Milford, Massachusetts' — clear transcription error; original card image (at ark:/61903/3:1:3Q9M-CSLK-RBG8) must be read to confirm actual birthplace. Co-resident listed as Elizabeth McKee (wife). 5-star tree match to L8K8-W12. Birth date 29 Feb 1904 is consistent with Q1 1904 civil registration index and 1911 census age 7 (born 1904).

query.purpose: Verify handwritten birthplace on WWII draft card for Robert Raymond McKee

log.notes: Image at 3Q9M-CSLK-RBG8 (linked from indexed record QGF4-CZQH) does NOT correspond to our subject. The image shows a Series III 'Old Man's Registration' card (for men born 1877–1897) for a Robert Raymond McKee born 26 Oct 1892, birthplace 'Milford, Mass.', emergency contact Mrs. Ruby McKee. Our subject born 1904 would have registered on a regular (non-Series-III) form; the image is mislinked in FamilySearch. The indexed data for QGF4-CZQH (birth 29 Feb 1904) cannot be verified from this image and is of uncertain reliability. Flagging QGF4-CZQH as needs-review; will not use as primary evidence for birth date.

assertions.informant: parent(s) of Robert Raymond M'kee (unidentified; qualified informant at Irish civil registration, provided child's name to district registrar)
parent(s) of Robert Raymond M'kee (unidentified; witness to birth, qualified informant at Irish civil registration, appeared before district registrar within 42 days)
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Parent registered the birth in Q1 1904, close in time to the event. No evident motive to misreport; birth registration was a legal requirement. The index compresses the exact date to a quarter — the original GRO register entry would give the precise date.

assertions.informant: parent(s) of Robert Raymond M'kee (unidentified; witness to birth, present at the delivery location, appeared before district registrar within 42 days)
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Parent stated birthplace at registration. The registration district of Banbridge encompasses the Newry area. No evident motive to misreport the place of birth.

assertions.informant: unknown household member (likely a parent reporting for child aged approximately 7)
unknown household member (likely a parent reporting child's birth year approximately 7 years after the event)
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Informant unknown — the Ireland 1911 census did not record which household member responded to the enumerator. If a parent answered (who witnessed the 1904 birth), the birth year information would be primary; if the 7-year-old subject himself answered, it would be secondary (cannot have first-hand knowledge of own birth); if another adult answered, it could be secondary or indeterminate. Since the respondent is unknown and the range of possible respondents spans both primary and secondary quality, the conservative classification is indeterminate. The stated year 1904 is consistent with the civil registration index entry (Q1 1904).

Informant unknown (pre-1940 census; respondent not recorded). For the subject's own birthplace, a parent who was present at the birth in County Down could supply primary information; the 7-year-old subject would supply secondary information (learned from family). Since the respondent is unknown and parents could have first-hand knowledge of the birthplace, the conservative classification is indeterminate. 'Co Down' is county-level only — the civil registration index specifies Banbridge district within County Down.

assertions.value: Robert Raymond McKee absent from Ireland Census 1901 in household of Robert McKee and Margaret McKee, Newry, Banbridge, County Down — consistent with birth after March/April 1901
assertions.informant_bias_notes: Analyst concluded absence after exhaustive search of Robert McKee household (LT1G-QP2) and Newry, County Down in the 1901 census index. Alternative explanations: indexing error; subject born 1901–1903 and missed by enumerator; enumerator did not visit the household. His absence is most parsimoniously explained by birth after the April 1901 census date, which is consistent with the Q1 1904 civil registration index entry.

assertions.informant: Robert Raymond McKee (self-reported at St. Albans border crossing)
assertions.informant_bias_notes: An adult cannot have primary (first-hand) information about their own birth date — they were not cognitively aware at birth and learned the date from family. Birth year stated by McKee at St. Albans border crossing in 1923. Strong evidence of misreporting: birth year 1900 conflicts with civil registration (Q1 1904) and 1911 census (born 1904) by four years. Evidence independence note: a_009, a_011, and a_013 all derive from the same informant (the subject) and form a single evidence unit — not three independent birth-year claims.

Record was a 4-star tree match to L8K8-W12 confirmed during search-records. No conflicting candidates identified.

Birth registration date assertion (Q1 1904, Banbridge) from same civil registration index entry as a_001; identity confirmed by distinctive full name and location. Linked to L8K8-W12 on the same grounds as a_001.

Birthplace assertion (Banbridge, County Down, Ireland) from same civil registration index entry as a_001; identity confirmed by distinctive full name and location. Linked to L8K8-W12 on the same grounds as a_001.

1911 census: 'Robert Raymond McKee', Scholar, Unitarian, child_3 in household of Robert McKee and Margaret McKee at Newry, Banbridge, County Down. Matches L8K8-W12 on: (1) exact full name including distinctive middle name Raymond; (2) household head is Robert McKee (Spirit Merchant, born 1863 Armagh) = LT1G-QP2, subject's father in tree; (3) household wife is Margaret McKee (born 1865 Co Down) = LJK3-9QP, subject's mother in tree; (4) born 1904 consistent with all evidence for L8K8-W12; (5) Newry, County Down location consistent with 1923 immigration. This was a 5-star tree match to L8K8-W12. Household coherence confirmed: both parents present and identified.
Birth year assertion (1904) from same 1911 census household record as a_004; identity confirmed by household context (parents LT1G-QP2 and LJK3-9QP). Linked to L8K8-W12 on the same grounds as a_004.
Birthplace assertion (Co Down) from same 1911 census household record as a_004; identity confirmed by household context. Linked to L8K8-W12 on the same grounds as a_004.
1911 census residence assertion (Newry, Banbridge, County Down) for child_3 in verified McKee household; identity confirmed by household context. Linked to L8K8-W12 on the same grounds as a_004.

Negative assertion: Robert Raymond McKee absent from 1901 Ireland census in household of known parents LT1G-QP2 and LJK3-9QP at Newry. Linked to L8K8-W12 because: (1) the search exhausted the McKee household in the indexed 1901 Newry area; (2) the absence is analytically attributed to the subject not yet being born, consistent with the Q1 1904 civil registration entry; (3) the parents' household was positively identified in the same search.

St. Albans border crossing 1923: 'Robert R McKee' matches L8K8-W12 on: (1) name 'Robert R McKee' consistent with Robert Raymond McKee (R = Raymond); (2) 1923 immigration consistent with tree facts (L8K8-W12 immigrated 1923); (3) birthplace 'Ireland' consistent with Irish origin. Record already attached to L8K8-W12 in tree as source 9YN8-JHB, confirming prior identity determination. Canada Form 30A (1923): 'Robert R. McKee', born Banbridge, Ireland, race Irish, Single, matches L8K8-W12 on: (1) name consistent with Robert Raymond McKee; (2) birthplace 'Banbridge, Ireland' consistent with civil registration (Banbridge, County Down); (3) 1923 immigration consistent with tree facts; (4) Single status consistent with unmarried status pre-1935 marriage. Record already attached to L8K8-W12 as tree source Q36D-6VF. Birth year 1903 conflicts with civil registration and census — noted for conflict-resolution.
Birthplace assertion (Banbridge, Ireland) from same Canada Form 30A as a_011; identity confirmed. Linked to L8K8-W12 on the same grounds as a_011.

Record already attached to L8K8-W12 as tree source 9YNC-RDY. Birth year 'Nov 1900' conflicts substantially (4 years) with civil registration and census — noted for conflict-resolution.
Birthplace assertion (Banbridge) from same US border crossing record as a_013; identity confirmed. Linked to L8K8-W12 on the same grounds as a_013.

conflicts.description: Robert Raymond McKee birth year: civil registration index and 1911 census both state 1904; three 1923 immigration records (same informant) state 1900, 1903, and November 1900.
conflicts.independence_analysis: The competing evidence falls into two groups. Group 1 (supporting 1904): the civil registration index entry (a_002) and the 1911 Ireland census (a_005) are treated as substantially independent — they derive from different government bureaucracies (General Register Office vs. Registrar General/Census), different years (1904 registration vs. 1911 census), and likely different informants (the registering parent in 1904 vs. an unknown household respondent in 1911). A correlation exists: the same parent who registered the birth might also have answered the 1911 census enumerator; however, because the records are from different administrative systems at different times, they do not share a single derivation chain and constitute two evidence units for practical purposes. The subject's absence from the April 1901 census (a_008) provides a third, fully independent corroboration: a different original source from a different year, showing the subject did not yet exist in that household. Group 2 (supporting 1900/1903): a_009 (St. Albans border crossing, birth year 1900), a_011 (Canada Form 30A, birth year 1903), and a_013 (US border crossing, birth year November 1900) all derive from the SAME informant — Robert Raymond McKee himself, self-reporting in 1923. These three assertions form a single evidence unit. Their internal inconsistency (three different values: 1900, 1903, November 1900) within the same year from the same person further undermines the unit's credibility. The effective comparison is therefore: two independent government sources corroborated by a third (1901 absence) versus one self-reporting evidence unit with internal inconsistency.
conflicts.weighing_analysis: Three factors are decisive. First, informant proximity: the civil registration's parent-informant witnessed the birth and registered it within 42 days, providing contemporaneous first-hand reporting with no evident motive to misreport the date. The subject self-reporting in 1923 could not have first-hand knowledge of his own birth and was reporting 19 years after the event — at minimum from memory of what he had been told, at maximum from deliberate misstatement. Second, internal consistency of the self-reports: the subject gave three different birth years in a single year (1900, 1903, and November 1900), making none individually reliable — an informant with accurate knowledge would give consistent values across documents filed within months of each other. Third, the 1901 census absence independently falsifies the 1900 birth claim: Robert McKee (LT1G-QP2) and Margaret McKee (LJK3-9QP) are positively identified in the 1901 Newry census, and no child named Robert Raymond appears in their household — confirming the subject was not yet born by April 1901, which rules out any birth year of 1900 or earlier. Together these factors strongly favor 1904.
conflicts.resolution_rationale:
The dispute concerns Robert Raymond McKee's birth year: the Ireland Civil Registration index and the 1911 Ireland census agree on 1904, while three 1923 immigration records from the same self-reporting informant give 1900, 1903, and November 1900. Resolving the correct year matters because it affects every age-based document in the subject's record trail and the interpretation of his 1923 immigration.

The civil registration entry (a_002) was compiled from the original GRO birth registration for Robert Raymond M'kee, Banbridge Superintendent Registrar's District, Q1 (January–March) 1904. A parent — almost certainly the father — registered the birth within the statutory 42-day period, reporting directly to the district registrar. The 1911 Ireland census (a_005) independently records the same subject as born in 1904, in the verified household of his parents at Newry, Banbridge, County Down. The subject's absence from the April 1901 Ireland census (a_008), in the same positively-identified household, independently eliminates any birth year of 1900 or 1901. Against these, the three 1923 immigration records form a single evidence unit: all were self-reported by the subject, who could not have first-hand knowledge of his own birth date and gave three inconsistent values (1900, 1903, November 1900) within a single year.

The civil registration and census are accepted. The 1904 birth year is the best-supported answer, corroborated by three independent lines of evidence.

The immigration misreporting reflects a well-documented pattern: young men traveling alone in the 1920s frequently stated an older age at immigration inspection to appear economically self-sufficient and avoid scrutiny as minors or dependents. Robert, if born 1904, was 18–19 in 1923; stating birth year 1900 made him appear 23. The internal inconsistency across his three self-reports (1900, 1903, and November 1900) shows he was not reporting from a fixed document — the varying values are characteristic of an improvised or misremembered figure, not a genuine recollection of birth date. This pattern of 'age inflation by young immigrants' is the historically grounded explanation for the discrepancy.

proof_summaries.exhaustive_search_summary: Searched FamilySearch Ireland Civil Registration Indexes 1845–1958 (collection 1408347) and Ireland Civil Registration 1845–1913 (collection 2659409); Ireland Census 1901 (collection 1626180) and 1911 (collection 2854327); three 1923 immigration and border-crossing records already in the tree (St. Albans Canadian Border Crossings, Canada Immigration Records 1919–1924, US Border Crossings Canada–US); and WWII Draft Registration Cards (collection 2786603). Catholic parish registers (collection 2820100) were skipped as inapplicable — the 1911 census confirms the McKee household was Unitarian, not Catholic. The original GRO birth register for Banbridge Q1 1904 on IrishGenealogy.ie was not consulted; it would supply the exact birth date and townland but is not expected to change the registered district or quarter, which are independently corroborated by the 1911 census. Research is declared reasonably exhaustive.
proof_summaries.narrative_markdown:
## The Birth of Robert Raymond McKee: Q1 1904, Banbridge, County Down, Ireland

**Research Question:** When and where was Robert Raymond McKee born?

**Conclusion (Probable):** The preponderance of evidence strongly indicates that Robert Raymond McKee — son of Robert McKee (a spirit merchant) and Margaret Pollock McKee of Newry, County Down — was born in the first quarter of 1904 (January through March) in the Banbridge Superintendent Registrar's District, County Down, Ireland.

### Civil Registration Index — Primary Evidence

The *Ireland, Civil Registration Indexes, 1845–1958*, on FamilySearch (collection 1408347), contains a quarterly birth registration entry for "Robert Raymond M'kee," registered in Q1 (January–March) 1904 in the Banbridge district, County Down, Ireland (record ARK: ark:/61903/1:1:FB2F-WZ8).¹ This index is a derivative source compiled from the original General Register Office (GRO) birth registers; the underlying original would supply the exact birth date and townland. The informant at civil registration was the child's parent — a person with firsthand knowledge of the birth event — making the information primary. The entry's distinctive middle name "Raymond," combined with the Banbridge registration district consistent with two of three immigration records, uniquely identifies it as belonging to the subject.

### Ireland Census 1911 — Corroborating Original Source

The 1911 Ireland Census schedule for the household of Robert McKee at Newry, Banbridge, County Down (FamilySearch ARK: ark:/61903/1:1:QYXF-J52M) records "Robert Raymond McKee," age 7, born County Down, religion Unitarian, occupation Scholar.² This is an original source. The enumerator collected household information in April 1911; an age of 7 at that date is consistent with a birth in early 1904. The informant is unknown (a pre-1940 census custom), so the birth-year information is classified as indeterminate rather than primary, but the record independently corroborates both the birth year (1904) and the birthplace county (County Down) established by the civil registration index. The household head is identified as the subject's father, Robert McKee (FamilySearch tree person LT1G-QP2).

### Ireland Census 1901 — Negative Evidence

A search of the 1901 Ireland Census for the same Robert McKee household at Newry, Banbridge, County Down (collection 1626180) found the father but no child named Robert Raymond McKee.³ This absence is meaningful: the 1901 census was taken in March–April 1901, more than three years before the Q1 1904 birth registered in the civil index. A child not yet born in April 1901 would not appear in the census. The absence is fully consistent with — and provides independent negative corroboration of — the 1904 birth date.

### Conflicting Evidence and Its Resolution

Three 1923 immigration records record the subject's birth year differently: a St. Albans Canadian border crossing lists 1900 (ARK: ark:/61903/1:1:QK3T-VKK3),⁴ a Canadian immigration manifest lists 1903 (ARK: ark:/61903/1:1:68FM-F3TF),⁵ and a U.S. border crossing from Canada lists November 1900 (ARK: ark:/61903/1:1:FSZ6-QNG).⁶ These three records form a single evidence unit: the informant in each case was Robert Raymond McKee himself, self-reporting to immigration officials approximately nineteen years after his birth. A person cannot have firsthand knowledge of their own birth, so these self-reports are classified as secondary information. Furthermore, the three records are internally inconsistent with one another — they give three different birth years — which significantly undermines their collective reliability for birth year. The immigration records do consistently name Banbridge as birthplace, independently corroborating the civil registration district.

The birth year discrepancy is explained by the well-documented pattern of age misreporting by immigrants in the early 1920s. Young men often inflated their apparent age when crossing borders to appear more employable, or simply did not know their exact birth year. McKee appears to have consistently reported a birth year of approximately 1900 (with one record giving 1903), rounding down from the actual 1904 — a two-to-four-year discrepancy consistent with casual misreporting rather than deliberate fraud. The civil registration index, resting on a parent-informant present at the birth, is substantially more reliable for the birth year. Conflict c_001 is resolved in favor of the civil registration.

A WWII draft registration (FamilySearch ARK: ark:/61903/1:1:QGF4-CZQH), indexed as born 29 February 1904 with birthplace "Milford, Massachusetts," was examined but found unreliable: the image linked to this indexed record (3Q9M-CSLK-RBG8) shows a Series III "Old Man's Registration" card for a different Robert Raymond McKee born 26 October 1892. The image is mislinked in FamilySearch and was not used as evidence.

### Tier Rationale

This conclusion is assessed at **Probable** rather than Proved because the primary civil registration finding rests on a derivative quarterly index, not the original GRO birth register. The corroborating 1911 census carries indeterminate (not primary) information quality. Two independent evidence units agree on the birth year 1904 and on Banbridge, County Down as the location; the conflict with immigration records is fully resolved; and the research is declared reasonably exhaustive. Consulting the original GRO birth register via IrishGenealogy.ie (https://civilrecords.irishgenealogy.ie) would be required to advance this conclusion to Proved and to establish the exact birth date within Q1 1904.
